challenges.title: Encode Decode
challenges.description:
Practice encoding and decoding strings to and from bytes.

Given `text = "café"`, create the following variables:
- `encoded` — the text encoded to bytes using UTF-8
- `decoded` — the encoded bytes decoded back to a string using UTF-8
- `ascii_safe` — the text encoded to ASCII with `errors='replace'`, then decoded back to ASCII
challenges.starter_code:
text = "café"

# Encode text to UTF-8 bytes
encoded = ...

# Decode the bytes back to a string
decoded = ...

# Encode to ASCII with errors='replace', then decode back
ascii_safe = ...
challenges.test_code:
assert isinstance(encoded, bytes), f"encoded should be bytes, got {type(encoded)}"
assert decoded == "café", f"decoded should be 'caf\u00e9', got '{decoded}'"
assert "?" in ascii_safe or "\\x" not in ascii_safe, f"ascii_safe should replace non-ASCII chars, got '{ascii_safe}'"
print("All tests passed!")
hints: Use text.encode('utf-8') to create the encoded bytes.
Use encoded.decode('utf-8') to decode back to a string.
Use text.encode('ascii', errors='replace').decode('ascii') for the ASCII-safe version.

challenges.title: Byte Length
challenges.description:
Write a function `byte_length(text, encoding='utf-8')` that returns the number of bytes needed to encode the given text in the specified encoding.

Note that characters like 'é' take more than one byte in UTF-8!
challenges.starter_code:
def byte_length(text, encoding='utf-8'):
    """Return the number of bytes needed to encode text in the given encoding."""
    # Encode the text and return the length of the resulting bytes
    ...
challenges.test_code:
assert byte_length("hello") == 5, f"Expected 5, got {byte_length('hello')}"
assert byte_length("café") == 5, f"Expected 5, got {byte_length('caf\u00e9')}"
assert byte_length("hello", 'ascii') == 5, f"Expected 5, got {byte_length('hello', 'ascii')}"
print("All tests passed!")
hints: Use text.encode(encoding) to get the bytes representation.
Use len() on the resulting bytes to count how many bytes there are.
The character 'é' is 2 bytes in UTF-8, so 'café' is 3 + 2 = 5 bytes.
